name: Mailjet Email API
what_it_does: EU-based transactional and marketing email API with real-time sending analytics, template engine, contact management, and strong GDPR compliance story for European applications.
use_cases: Sending transactional emails (confirmations, notifications, alerts) with real-time delivery tracking
Marketing email campaigns with contact list management and segmentation
EU-compliant email delivery where data residency and GDPR are primary concerns
Inbound email parsing via webhooks for agent-driven email triage workflows
Multi-sender transactional email from a single account using sub-accounts
not_for: Ultra-high-volume transactional email where deliverability is paramount (Postmark or SendGrid Pro have stronger infrastructure)
Teams needing an MCP server or deep AI agent integrations out of the box
Complex multi-step marketing automation workflows (ActiveCampaign or Klaviyo are better)
best_when: European SaaS products need a GDPR-compliant email API with EU data residency, reasonable pricing, and solid deliverability without the enterprise overhead of AWS SES.
avoid_when: Your primary concern is maximum transactional deliverability at scale — Postmark and SendGrid Pro have deeper infrastructure for high-stakes transactional email.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth using API Key as username and Secret Key as password. Two-key model (API Key + Secret Key) per account. No granular scope support — each key pair has full account access. Sub-account API keys available for multi-tenant setups. Agent-friendly with no token expiry.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 200 emails/day (6,000/month) on the free tier; includes transactional and marketing emails, API access
pricing.paid_tiers: Essential from $15/month (15K emails); Premium from $25/month (15K emails, automation, segmentation); Custom enterprise
pricing.notes: Free tier is more generous than SendGrid's 100/day limit. EU company (acquired by Sinch in 2022). Pricing is competitive especially for EU-based companies. No per-seat pricing — volume-based only.

performance.rate_limits: ~300 requests/second on free; higher on paid; bulk sending uses async batch endpoints

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Mailjet returns JSON error objects with StatusCode, ErrorMessage, and ErrorIdentifier. Error detail level is adequate but not as informative as SendGrid or Postmark. Some validation errors lack field-level specificity. Async sending errors only surface via Event Webhooks.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Each send call creates a new message. Agents must implement external state tracking to prevent duplicate sends on retry, identical to SendGrid's approach.
known_agent_gotchas: Two-credential auth (API Key + Secret Key) differs from typical single-key API patterns — pass both as HTTP Basic auth, not as header values
Domain and sender validation required — new sender addresses need verification before sending, which can delay first-time agent deployments
No idempotency keys — retrying a failed send creates duplicate emails; agents need external dedup state
Event Webhook payloads are arrays of events — handlers must process batch payloads, not single-event objects
Contact management API and transactional send API have separate endpoint bases (v3/REST vs v3.1/send) — easy to mix up
Inbound email parsing requires MX record configuration — not self-service; involves DNS setup
Free tier rate limits lower than paid tiers — test at realistic volumes before production deployment

security_score_components.security_notes: Email API. API key + secret. Sending email at scale — protect from abuse. GDPR contact management. SPF/DKIM/DMARC setup required for deliverability.
